text.title: ai-research-tools Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-research-tools as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using one general-purpose chat model for all research stages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Interview-analysis, competitor-intel, and market-data stages each require specialist tooling; a single LLM cannot substitute all three and produces shallow, biased, citation-poor results.

failure-mode.description: Trusting AI output without human analysis.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The era of "best AI assistant" as a single winner is over (2026). The right assistant is task-matched; no tool triangulates all stages autonomously — human curation between stages is mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Perplexity API results differ from the web UI (no "Pro Search" toggle in API v1; use search_recency_filter instead).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Testing only the web UI and then relying on the API will produce coverage gaps; both must be validated before committing to API-only flow.

failure-mode.description: Synthesis agents lose track of source attribution when chaining multiple tool calls without explicit metadata passthrough.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Source metadata must be passed explicitly through every pipeline step, or the final report cannot be audited for citation quality.

failure-mode.description: Free-tier rate limits (Google Trends, SerpAPI) silently truncate results without error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Truncated results look complete but are not; add explicit error handling and result-count validation for every rate-limited tool.
